title: Internationalisation of corporate activity and competitiveness of the European economy: some policy implications
summary_en: Final policy paper of the MicroDyn FP7 project. Synthesises the project's firm-level evidence on European internationalisation and competitiveness, with a particular focus on the productivity gains from FDI, importing, and exporting. Hungarian micro-data is used as one of the key empirical settings.
policy_relevance: Frame for European Commission Enterprise DG and for Hungarian FDI promotion (HIPA): the policy mix should target multiple internationalisation modes (import as well as export, FDI both in and out), not just headline export volumes. Caveat: an FP7 synthesis paper, so the specific recommendations are hedged.
